Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (born 15 June 1953) is a Chinese politician and leader, who since 2012 has served as General Secretary of the Communist Party of China (CCP) and Chairman of the Party’s Central Military Commission (CMC). He has also been President of China and Chairman of the National Military Commission since 2013. He is considered the leader of China’s fifth generation of leadership.
Xi’s father Xi Zhongxun was a Red Army hero, and from his mother’s second marriage Xi was born in Beijing. During the Cultural Revolution in June 1966, due to opposition against his father, he was sent to Liangjiahe village in Shaanxi, where he had to live in a cave. In his early life he failed several times to join the Communist Party of China, but eventually he made a new beginning in his life.
Xi studied chemical engineering at Tsinghua University, where he studied as a worker-peasant-soldier student. After his political rise, he increased his moral influence in China’s coastal provinces. From 1999 to 2002 he was Governor of Fujian, and from 2002 to 2007 he was Governor and Party Secretary of Zhejiang.
In 2012 Xi began his leadership as General Secretary of the Communist Party of China, after which in 2013 he became President of China. During his leadership, China’s economic development, social reforms and international relations have made significant contributions. He is considered a major leader for China’s modernization and expansion of its power in the world.
Barack Obama:
Biography of Barack Obama

Barack Hasan Obama II (8 August, 1961) is an American politician, who served as the 44th President of America from 2009 to 2017. He became the first African-American President of America. Obama’s birthplace was in Honolulu, Hawaii, where his father came from Kenya and mother was a British American of Hawaii. His father’s name was Barack Hasan Obama Junior, who was an African politician, while his mother Mary Obama was an American citizen.

Obama obtained a Bachelor of Arts degree in political science from Columbia University in 1983, where he wrote about the experiences of his life. He worked as a community organizer in Chicago, where he gained more awareness about his political interest. In 1988 he took admission in Harvard Law School, where he served as the first African-American president of the Harvard Law Review.

From 1997 to 2004 Obama was a member of the state parliament of Illinois, where he worked on policies related to education, health and economy. In 2004 he contested election as a member of the parliament of Illinois and won. In 2008 he participated in the election of the President of America and won, after which he contested election for the claim of President in 2009.

During Obama’s presidency, policies were made in America on financial crisis, rights of African-American tribe, and international relations. Under his leadership America brought policies for economic recovery after its financial crisis. During his presidency America adopted a new approach in its foreign relations, in which relations were increased with Africa, Asia and Europe.

In 2017 at the end of Obama’s presidency, after the end of his political tenure he of his presidential office
